_note: Refreshed by the refresh-sports-feed scheduled task. Each item is {headline, url, source, published (ISO8601), snark}. Snark voice: Barstool-adjacent guy-at-the-bar, crass, horny, cocky, zero filler. One sentence. No slurs, no punching down at specific real people in a harmful way. Punch sideways (at media, front offices, rival fanbases) and up (at ownership). Keep it Boston.

items.headline: Red Sox vs Phillies preview: Ranger Suarez faces former team Thursday at 6:45 PM
items.snark: Suarez gets to pitch against Philly after they let him walk, awkward family dinner energy incoming.

items.headline: Garrett Crochet throws 20-pitch bullpen, could return late May from shoulder injury
items.snark: Crochet threw off a mound without his arm falling off, parade scheduled for City Hall Plaza.

items.headline: Red Sox using ABS challenge system less than any team except one
items.snark: Sox refusing to tap their helmets like they're allergic to technology that might actually help them win.

items.headline: Ceddanne Rafaela's pinch-hit two-run homer gives Red Sox 3-1 win over Phillies
items.snark: Rafaela came off the bench and crushed one like a guy who remembered he's actually good at baseball.

items.headline: Trevor Story blasts 424-foot homer as Red Sox offense shows signs of life
items.snark: Story hit a ball 424 feet and the Sox celebrated like they just cured cancer instead of scoring three runs.

items.headline: Patriots schedule release set for 8 PM tonight, four of 17 games already leaked
items.snark: NFL pretending schedule release is a surprise while every game leaks like a busted pipe, classic.

items.headline: Karon Prunty's senior year surge at Wake Forest landed him on Patriots draft board
items.snark: Cornerback finally figured it out as a super senior, Belichick would've cut him three times already.

items.headline: Bills avoid international game while Patriots forced to schlep to Munich
items.snark: Buffalo stays home while the Pats rack up Delta miles, Goodell clearly still salty about Deflategate.

items.headline: Patriots open 2026 season with Super Bowl rematch at Seahawks on Sept. 9
items.snark: Nothing says revenge tour like watching your opponent raise a banner before kickoff, smart scheduling.

items.headline: Patriots to face Lions in Munich Week 10 on Nov. 15, second Germany trip in four years
items.snark: Pats heading back to Germany because apparently losing to Indy in Frankfurt wasn't enough punishment.

items.headline: Patriots WR Kyle Williams sticking with No. 18, fueling Stefon Diggs reunion rumors
items.snark: Williams changed his mind on jersey numbers, fans convinced this means Diggs is coming, delusion thriving.

items.headline: Patriots to face Lions in Munich Week 10 international game on November 15
items.snark: Third Germany trip in four years because nothing says AFC champs like schlepping to Bavaria every other season.
